teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sl/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then l. Help them blend through: s-l-a-m → slam. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-l-a-m → Blend the opening cluster: sl-a-m → slam
s-l-u-g → Say each sound: /s/-/l/-/u/-/g/. Blend: slug
s-l-i-m → Say each sound: /s/-/l/-/i/-/m/. Blend: slim
s-l-o-t → Say each sound: /s/-/l/-/o/-/t/. Blend: slot

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Said' looks like s-ai-d but we say 'sed'. Learn it by sight.

story: He said the slim slug did a slam. The slug slid into the slot and got stuck!

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sl/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then l. Help them blend through: s-l-a-p → slap. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-l-a-p → Blend the opening cluster: sl-a-p → slap
s-l-e-d → Say each sound: /s/-/l/-/e/-/d/. Blend: sled
s-l-i-p → Say each sound: /s/-/l/-/i/-/p/. Blend: slip
s-l-o-b → Say each sound: /s/-/l/-/o/-/b/. Blend: slob

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Have' — the 'a' says its short sound and the 'e' is silent. Learn it by sight.

story: I have a sled! The slob did slip and slap the sled. What a mess!

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sl/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then l. Help them blend through: s-l-i-t → slit. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-l-i-t → Blend the opening cluster: sl-i-t → slit
s-l-u-m → Say each sound: /s/-/l/-/u/-/m/. Blend: slum
s-l-i-d → Say each sound: /s/-/l/-/i/-/d/. Blend: slid
s-l-u-g → Say each sound: /s/-/l/-/u/-/g/. Blend: slug

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Like' — the 'i' says its name and the 'e' is silent. Learn it by sight.

story: I like this slug! The slug slid up to a slit in the wall. It crept into the slum and hid.

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sm/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then m. Help them blend through: s-m-e-ll → smell. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-m-e-ll → Blend the opening cluster: sm-e-ll → smell
s-m-o-g → Say each sound: /s/-/m/-/o/-/g/. Blend: smog
s-m-a-sh → Blend the opening cluster: sm-a-sh → smash
s-m-u-g → Say each sound: /s/-/m/-/u/-/g/. Blend: smug

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'So' — the 'o' says its name. Learn it by sight.

story: It was so smoggy! I could smell the smog. The smug cat did smash a cup and ran off.

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sm/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then m. Help them blend through: s-m-a-ll → small. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-m-a-ll → Blend the opening cluster: sm-a-ll → small
s-m-a-sh → Blend the opening cluster: sm-a-sh → smash
s-m-e-ll → Blend the opening cluster: sm-e-ll → smell
s-m-o-g → Say each sound: /s/-/m/-/o/-/g/. Blend: smog

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Do' — the 'o' says 'oo'. We say 'doo'. Learn it by sight.

story: What do you smell? A small bug did smash into the smog. It was such a funny little crash!

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sn/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then n. Help them blend through: s-n-a-p → snap. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-n-a-p → Blend the opening cluster: sn-a-p → snap
s-n-a-g → Say each sound: /s/-/n/-/a/-/g/. Blend: snag
s-n-i-p → Say each sound: /s/-/n/-/i/-/p/. Blend: snip
s-n-u-g → Say each sound: /s/-/n/-/u/-/g/. Blend: snug

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Some' — the 'o' says 'u' and the 'e' is silent. We say 'sum'. Learn it by sight.

story: Can you snip some ribbon? Snap! There is a snag. The cat is snug in the ribbon now!

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sn/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then n. Help them blend through: s-n-i-ff → sniff. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-n-i-ff → Blend the opening cluster: sn-i-ff → sniff
s-n-o-b → Say each sound: /s/-/n/-/o/-/b/. Blend: snob
s-n-a-p → Say each sound: /s/-/n/-/a/-/p/. Blend: snap
s-n-u-g → Say each sound: /s/-/n/-/u/-/g/. Blend: snug

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'o' says 'u' and the 'e' is silent. We say 'cum'. Learn it by sight.

story: Come and have a sniff! The snob did snap at the dog, but the dog was all snug in its bed.

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sp/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then p. Help them blend through: s-p-o-t → spot. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-p-o-t → Blend the opening cluster: sp-o-t → spot
s-p-i-n → Say each sound: /s/-/p/-/i/-/n/. Blend: spin
s-p-a-n → Say each sound: /s/-/p/-/a/-/n/. Blend: span
s-p-i-t → Say each sound: /s/-/p/-/i/-/t/. Blend: spit

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Were' does not sound like it looks. We say 'wur'. Learn it by sight.

story: We were at the park. I could spot a top that did spin and spin. It went a big span across the grass!

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sp/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then p. Help them blend through: s-p-e-ll → spell. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-p-e-ll → Blend the opening cluster: sp-e-ll → spell
s-p-u-d → Say each sound: /s/-/p/-/u/-/d/. Blend: spud
s-p-e-d → Say each sound: /s/-/p/-/e/-/d/. Blend: sped
s-p-i-ll → Blend the opening cluster: sp-i-ll → spill

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'There' — the 'th' and 'ere' are tricky. We say 'thair'. Learn it by sight.

story: Look over there! I sped to the kitchen and did spill the spud soup. Can you spell 'spud'?

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sp/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then p. Help them blend through: s-p-u-n → spun. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-p-u-n → Blend the opening cluster: sp-u-n → spun
s-p-o-t → Say each sound: /s/-/p/-/o/-/t/. Blend: spot
s-p-i-n → Say each sound: /s/-/p/-/i/-/n/. Blend: spin
s-p-u-d → Say each sound: /s/-/p/-/u/-/d/. Blend: spud

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Little' — the 'le' at the end is tricky. Learn it by sight.

story: The little top spun and spun on the spot. It did spin so fast! Then it hit a spud and fell down.

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /st/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then t. Help them blend through: s-t-o-p → stop. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-t-o-p → Blend the opening cluster: st-o-p → stop
s-t-a-b → Say each sound: /s/-/t/-/a/-/b/. Blend: stab
s-t-e-m → Say each sound: /s/-/t/-/e/-/m/. Blend: stem
s-t-e-p → Say each sound: /s/-/t/-/e/-/p/. Blend: step

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'One' looks like it should rhyme with 'bone' but we say 'wun'. Learn it by sight.

story: One step at a time! Stop and look at the stem of the big plant. Do not stab it with a stick!

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /st/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then t. Help them blend through: s-t-i-ck → stick. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-t-i-ck → Blend the opening cluster: st-i-ck → stick
s-t-i-ff → Blend the opening cluster: st-i-ff → stiff
s-t-i-ll → Blend the opening cluster: st-i-ll → still
s-t-u-b → Say each sound: /s/-/t/-/u/-/b/. Blend: stub

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'When' — the 'wh' makes a /w/ sound. Learn it by sight.

story: When I pick up a stick it is stiff and still. I stub my toe on a log — ouch!

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /st/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then t. Help them blend through: s-t-u-ff → stuff. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-t-u-ff → Blend the opening cluster: st-u-ff → stuff
s-t-u-ng → Blend the opening cluster: st-u-ng → stung
s-t-i-n-k → Five sounds! Blend the opening cluster: st-i-nk → stink
s-t-u-d → Say each sound: /s/-/t/-/u/-/d/. Blend: stud

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Out' — the 'ou' makes an /ow/ sound. Learn it by sight.

story: Get that stuff out! It got stung by a bee and now it does stink. What a stud that brave bug was!

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /st/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then t. Help them blend through: s-t-a-m-p → stamp. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-t-a-m-p → Five sounds! Blend the opening cluster: st-a-mp → stamp
s-t-a-n-d → Five sounds! Blend the opening cluster: st-a-nd → stand
s-t-o-m-p → Five sounds! Blend the opening cluster: st-o-mp → stomp
s-t-u-m-p → Five sounds! Blend the opening cluster: st-u-mp → stump

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'What' — the 'wh' says /w/ and the 'a' says /o/. We say 'wot'. Learn it by sight.

story: What a stomp! Stand on the stump and stamp your feet. Stomp, stomp, stomp! The sting of the cold did not stop us.
